title: euphorbia grandicornis subsp. sejuncta
intro: Euphorbia grandicornis subsp. sejuncta, also known as the 'candelabra tree' or 'candelabra euphorbia', is a succulent plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive, branching habit and red, pointed spines. The plant is used in traditional medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its chemical compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Euphorbia grandicornis subsp. sejuncta are said to include the reduction of inflammation, improvement of skin conditions, and support for the immune system. Traditional herbal preparations involving this plant include infusions and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments in folk medicine.
